title: Claude Fable 5 is Banned... Do THIS Right Now
summary: A video argues that relying on cloud-hosted AI models is risky because they can be withdrawn without notice, citing Claude Fable 5's removal. The presenter advocates running open-source models locally using Ollama and building a free NotebookLM alternative with open notebook for data independence and model access security.
writeup: The video uses the removal of Claude Fable 5 to argue that cloud-hosted AI models carry a structural risk: they can be withdrawn without notice, breaking workflows that depend on them. It covers running open-source foundation models locally with Ollama and building a free NotebookLM alternative using open notebook, positioning local inference as a path to data independence and continued model access. The core takeaway is that local hosting removes reliance on a provider's decision to keep a given model available.
